metadata.notes: Asynchronous notification of actors after reversal. Special handling for conflicting requests.

inputs.vague: The Spirit Director changes their mind about an appearance request, so they indicate that they want to reverse a prior approval or rejection. The system then processes this change, updating the request's status and making necessary adjustments to the calendar.
inputs.detailed:
Primary Actor:
Spirit Director
Secondary Actors:
Customer, SuperFrog Student
Trigger:
The Spirit Director indicates to reverse the approval/rejection decision of an appearance request.
Description:
The Spirit Director wants to reverse the approval/rejection decision of an appearance request, so that she has an opportunity to change her mind for a request. See BR-9.
Preconditions:
PRE-1. There exists at least one “Approved” or “Rejected” request in the System.
PRE-2. The Spirit Director is logged into the System.
PRE-3. The User has the “admin” privilege.
Postconditions:
POST-1. The appearance request is updated in the System with the modified status.
POST-2. If a “Rejected” appearance request is approved, the appearance is added to the Spirit Team calendar so that the requested time becomes unavailable to future appearance requests.
POST-3. If an “Approved” appearance request is rejected, the request is removed from the Spirit Team calendar so that the requested time becomes available again to future appearance requests.
Main Success Scenario:
The Spirit Director indicates to reverse the approval/rejection decision of an appearance request.
The Spirit Director views the details of this appearance request through UC-7: View an appearance request.
The Spirit Director chooses to reverse the approval/rejection decision of this appearance request.

Step 4-8: rejecting an “Approved” appearance request. 
Step 9-14:approving a “Rejected” appearance request.

The Spirit Director chooses to reject the request.
The System asks the Spirit Director to confirm the rejection.
The Spirit Director confirms the rejection and enters a reason.
The System updates the status of the request from “Approved” to “Rejected”.
The System removes the request from the Spirit Team calendar.

The Spirit Director chooses to approve the request.
The System validates the approval.
The System asks the Spirit Director to confirm the approval.
The Spirit Director confirms the approval.
The System updates the status of the request from “Rejected” to “Approved”.
The System adds the request to the Spirit Team calendar.

The System notifies relevant actors about the reversal of the request according to the “Notification” defined in the Associated Information of this use case.
Use case ends.
Extensions:
10a. There exists conflicting “Approved” request on the Spirit Team calendar:
10a1. The System alerts and shows the conflicts.
10a2. Use case ends.

groundTruth.name: Reverse Approval/Rejection of Appearance Request
groundTruth.summary: The Spirit Director reverses the approval or rejection decision of an appearance request.
preconditions: There exists at least one 'Approved' or 'Rejected' request in the System.
The Spirit Director is logged into the System.

steps.description: Indicates to reverse the approval/rejection decision of an appearance request.
Views the details of this appearance request.
Chooses to reverse the approval/rejection decision of this appearance request.
Notifies relevant actors about the reversal of the request.
Chooses to reject the request that was previously approved.
Asks the Spirit Director to confirm the rejection.
Confirms the rejection and enters a reason.
Updates the status of the request from 'Approved' to 'Rejected'.
Removes the request from the Spirit Team calendar.
Chooses to approve the request that was previously rejected.
Asks the Spirit Director to confirm the approval.
Updates the status of the request from 'Rejected' to 'Approved'.
Adds the request to the Spirit Team calendar.
